comment: Drifella Source Checks — friendly trivia encounters. v1 ships Morningside's 8 (6 visible, 2 hidden); the other 87 are allocated in drifella-source-check-manifest.json awaiting the placement+authoring pass. Questions follow the witnessed-only doctrine; in every multipleChoice4 the officialIndex option sounds institutional and is always wrong. Placements probe-verified walkable 2026-07-03.

personality.bit: A return-desk librarian who treats every rumor like an overdue book.

entryPrompt: The Drifella looks at you the way a librarian looks at a returning book.
"Source Check? It's friendly. Mostly questions. The questions are also friendly."

pool.prompt: The card that arrived before breakfast asked Bosch's permission first.
pool.failLine: "It didn't even knock. Cards never knock."

pool.prompt: The category box on the leaked card was checked—
pool.failLine: "Close. Closeness is not a category."

pool.prompt: Bosch kept the damaged original card.
pool.failLine: "He did. Damage is a kind of proof."

pool.prompt: The card-stock trail out of Bosch's block ran—
pool.failLine: "West. The crowd deals faces west."

pool.prompt: A 'readymade' in art is—
options: An ordinary object declared to be art
pool.failLine: "The library reshelves it under: object, reclassified."

pool.prompt: A signed urinal has hung in serious art museums.
pool.failLine: "Duchamp's 'Fountain.' Filed under: yes, really."

cleared: "The card is warm. Filed under: yours, with witnesses."
"Perfect return. No fine, just the card."
failed: "No harm done. Filed under: come back with steadier hands."
"The questions miss you already. Very embarrassing for them."
alreadyCleared: "We've met. The binder remembers even when you don't."
"Filed under: already returned."

personality.bit: A ceremonial notary who stamps air, silence, and mild civic dread.

entryPrompt: This Drifella stamps the air twice before speaking, notarizing nothing.
"State your attention for the record. Source Check?"

pool.prompt: The arcade crowd deals in—
pool.failLine: "Faces. I cannot notarize 'high scores.'"

pool.prompt: The Doxx Clique crowds you like an angry mob.
pool.failLine: "No. It ARRANGES itself. Smiling. That's worse, and it's on the record."

pool.prompt: A stamped exit is the only legal way out of Morningside.
pool.failLine: "FALSE, thank goodness. Unprocessed departures remain a civic right."

pool.prompt: What did the leaked card say under Bosch's almost-right face?
options: "I said it first, so I don't have to mean it."
pool.failLine: "He never said it. That was the whole problem. Stamped: problem."

pool.prompt: Marcel Duchamp turned a urinal into art mostly by—
options: Signing it and giving it a title
pool.failLine: "A signature. My whole trade. *stamp*"

pool.prompt: An artist can turn a thing into art just by deciding it is.
pool.failLine: *stamp* "Declared, therefore notarized."

correct: "Attention verified. Initial here in your mind."
cleared: "Perfect record. Card issued; pride unofficially attached."
*stamp* "Case closed without incident. Rare."
failed: "I cannot notarize that. Return when rested."
*stamp?* "No. Even the stamp hesitated."
alreadyCleared: "Your file is closed and beautiful. Do not reopen it."
*stamp* "Previously witnessed."

personality.bit: A roadside clerk who files questions by patience, not date.

entryPrompt: The Drifella by the road has been watching the precinct with great patience.
"They file people in there. I file questions. Want some?"

pool.prompt: The building the town calls THE PRECINCT is where—
options: The police keep the roadblock's paperwork
pool.failLine: "Paperwork. It's always paperwork."

pool.prompt: The road north opens the moment you ask politely.
pool.failLine: "Politeness is not a route permit. The route opens when the route opens."

pool.prompt: A voice from the next room worried about a card before breakfast.
pool.failLine: "It had panic under the paint. You were there. Listen harder next time."

pool.prompt: Battle winnings in this town travel—
options: Wired to your account, by the connect
pool.failLine: "Wired. The connect handles it. Check the account, not the pocket."

pool.prompt: A perfect copy of a painting is worth less than the original because we care about—
pool.failLine: "Provenance. I file everything by where it's been."

pool.prompt: The 'aura' of an original artwork is—
options: A presence the copy can't hold
pool.failLine: "The part the copy never gets. Sound familiar?"

correct: "You watch this town like I do. Unsettling. Good."
cleared: "A perfect file. Take the card before the precinct hears."
"Closed folder. Clean tab. Suspiciously satisfying."
failed: "Misfiled. Happens to the best cabinets. Come back around."
"Wrong drawer. The label forgives you."
alreadyCleared: "Your file is the one I show new questions as an example."
"Already filed. The precinct still has not noticed."

personality.bit: A gentle corner-grader giving the town quiet marks in pencil.

entryPrompt: A Drifella on the south road, facing town, as if grading it gently.
"Everything here is a question if you stand still long enough. Shall we?"

pool.prompt: A Dox Sheet proves Bosch is privately understood.
pool.failLine: "It proves he is publicly ASSEMBLED. Different thing. Sadder thing."

pool.prompt: What kind of proof does the Source Pocket keep?
pool.failLine: "Specificity. Alive. You can tell because it wriggles."

pool.prompt: The mailbox receipt was addressed to Bosch.
pool.failLine: "Addressed to DOX SHEET. The mail knows something we don't."

pool.prompt: The kid on the stoop had been waiting—
pool.failLine: "His whole short life. He said so. Kids keep excellent records."

pool.prompt: Expressionism cares most about—
pool.failLine: "Feeling first. Likeness is a formality."

pool.prompt: Van Gogh's swirling night skies aim to copy exactly how a sky looks.
pool.failLine: "They show what it FELT like. Grade: felt."

correct: "You stood still long enough. It shows."
"Full credit for noticing the sad part."
cleared: "Full marks. The south road approves of almost nothing."
"The pencil rests. Take the card before it grades me."
failed: "The road will still be here. So will the questions."
"Soft correction. No permanent mark."
alreadyCleared: "Go on. Other corners are waiting to be noticed."
"Already graded. Surprisingly legible."

personality.bit: A roadside itinerary clerk who treats memory like luggage with a tag.

entryPrompt: This Drifella stands where the road starts thinking about leaving town.
"Travelers remember the strangest things. Prove it?"

pool.prompt: The north road was open from the very first morning.
pool.failLine: "Blocked. Politely, officially, thoroughly blocked."

pool.prompt: Who deals faces at the west end of town?
pool.failLine: "The arcade crowd. There is no Bureau of Likeness. Yet."

pool.prompt: A leaked version of Bosch was reported better behaved and earlier to breakfast.
pool.failLine: "Earlier to breakfast! The audacity of the punctual."

pool.prompt: The face on the leaked card was wrong the way—
options: A bad memory is almost right
pool.failLine: "Almost right. That's the dangerous kind of wrong."

pool.prompt: The Dada movement was deliberately—
pool.failLine: "Nonsense on purpose. Pack it with the rest."

pool.prompt: 'Avant-garde' means art that is—
pool.failLine: "Ahead of the route. It always is."

correct: "Mileage note: you did walk this road."
cleared: "Take the card. Show it to the horizon when you get there."
"Route complete. The road signs off in pencil."
failed: "The road forgives worse itineraries."
"Wrong turn. Leave town in your head, then come back."
alreadyCleared: "Safe travels. Watch for the ones who hide."
"Mileage note: already witnessed."

personality.bit: An obsessive window-counter who suspects architecture of perjury.

entryPrompt: A Drifella idles by the west block, counting windows. It waves without looking.
"Forty-one windows. Zero of them honest. Anyway — Source Check?"

pool.prompt: Talking to someone in this town requires standing near them first.
pool.failLine: "Proximity, then words. The town runs on this and little else."

pool.prompt: A present box in this world, once opened—
pool.failLine: "Stays opened. Honesty is rare; boxes have it."

pool.prompt: Signs in Morningside can be read by pressing the talk button at them.
pool.failLine: "They wait YEARS to be read. Have a heart."

pool.prompt: The household voice heard on the first morning had—
pool.failLine: "Panic. Under the paint. You could hear the brush strokes."

pool.prompt: A Mark Rothko painting is mostly—
pool.failLine: "Color, counted in big honest blocks."

pool.prompt: Abstract art is required to look like a real thing.
pool.failLine: "Not required to resemble anything. The windows agree."

correct: "Forty-two honest things in town now. You're one."
"One pane stopped lying when you said that."
cleared: "Full count. The card is yours; I saw it arrive first."
"Forty-one windows, one clean answer sheet."
failed: "Off by one. Everything here is off by one. Come back."
"A window coughed when you said that. Bad sign."
alreadyCleared: "Still counting. Still forty-one windows. Still fond of you."
"Already cleared. The windows refuse to improve."

personality.bit: A quiet-side precinct lurker who rewards good peripheral suspicion.

hints.page: Something patient lives behind the precinct. It grades kindly, my aunt says. My aunt is never wrong twice.

entryPrompt: Tucked behind the precinct, a Drifella nods as if you were expected.
"You found the quiet side of the building. That was the first question. You passed."

pool.prompt: The best place to stand, in this town, is—
pool.failLine: "Behind things. Beside things. The record has terrible peripheral vision."

pool.prompt: Every Drifella in this town stands on the main road.
pool.failLine: "Present company behind the building, as evidence."

pool.prompt: The Doxx Clique already knew Bosch's name before he introduced himself.
pool.failLine: "Like a search result that already knows your name. You shivered. I saw."

pool.prompt: What does a hidden Drifella reward?
pool.failLine: "Curiosity. You HAD it a minute ago — it's how you got here."

pool.prompt: Sol LeWitt said the idea itself can be 'a machine that makes the—'
pool.failLine: "The idea does the work. You found the idea behind the wall."

pool.prompt: In conceptual art, the idea can matter more than the finished object.
pool.failLine: "The thought is the piece. The rest is quiet-side detail."

correct: "The building approves. Buildings talk to me."
"The record looked forward. You looked around."
cleared: "A holo card, for someone who looks behind things. Fitting."
"Curiosity logged. Quiet side rewards its own."
failed: "Even the quiet side has off days. Circle the block."
"The building winced softly. Try again after shade."
alreadyCleared: "Shh. Others haven't found me yet. Let them earn it."
"Already rewarded. Stay peripheral."

personality.bit: A hotel-side hummer preserving a tune no camera can prove.

hints.page: The hotel has a side nobody photographs. Something up there hums when the town gets quiet.

entryPrompt: On the hotel's unphotographed side, a Drifella hums a tune with no public version.
"You hear it too? Good. The questions go better with the hum."

pool.prompt: The hotel will rest your whole party for free.
pool.failLine: "Rest is real, and real things cost. The hum is free. Only the hum."

pool.prompt: Bosch pocketed the damaged card because—
options: Unfiled and unflattering, it was HIS
Municipal code requires retention of documents
pool.failLine: "His. Damage and all. ESPECIALLY the damage."

pool.prompt: A Source Check hurts.
pool.failLine: "Has any of this hurt? Be honest. You're doing great."

pool.prompt: What arrives before you do, if you're not careful?
pool.failLine: "The leak. It's faster because it's lighter. It carries none of the truth."

pool.prompt: John Cage's '4'33"' has the performer—
options: Sit and play nothing, so you hear the room
pool.failLine: "Silence, framed. The room hums. So do I."

pool.prompt: A 'field recording' captures—
options: The real sounds of an actual place
A form filled out in a field
pool.failLine: "The place, humming to itself. No public version."

pool.prompt: Silence itself can be the subject of a piece of music.
pool.failLine: "Ask Cage. Ask the unphotographed side of the hotel."

correct: "No public version, but that note was true."
cleared: "Take the card. It hums a little if you hold it right."
"Clean chord. Holo enough for the hotel's bad side."
failed: "The hum drops a note when that happens. Come restore it."
"Flat note. Still yours to tune."
alreadyCleared: "Hum with me a while. No questions. We've done those."
"Already in tune. The hotel side keeps it quiet."

personality.bit: A sound-check tech for The Venue who treats every rumor like feedback to be EQ'd out.

entryPrompt: The Drifella taps an invisible microphone twice and waits for the room to settle.
"Source Check. Two questions, no reverb. If your story clips, we cut the feed."

pool.prompt: The card that started all this arrived at Bosch's place before breakfast.
pool.failLine: "Before breakfast. Trouble skips meals."

pool.prompt: The category box checked on that first leaked card read—
pool.failLine: "VESSEL. Nobody checks the box they'd choose."

pool.prompt: Morningside's card-stock trail ran west, toward the arcade.
pool.failLine: "West. The crowd deals faces west."

pool.prompt: Andy Warhol got famous for painting, over and over, a can of—
pool.failLine: "Soup. Repetition is a kind of volume."

pool.prompt: Erik Satie wrote 'furniture music' — music designed to be ignored on purpose.
pool.failLine: "On purpose. The bravest setting is background."

pool.prompt: A sound check exists mostly to protect—
pool.failLine: "Ears. Everything else heals."

cleared: "Take the card. It's mixed a little hot, like everything good."
"Sourced and sound. Tell the door I said so."
failed: "Feedback. Walk it off and come re-level."
"We'll fix it in the next pass. There's always a next pass."
alreadyCleared: "You're on the list. The list is me."
"Check's done. Enjoy the show that is: everything."

personality.bit: A ticket-stub archivist who believes every event leaves a paper soul.

entryPrompt: The Drifella fans out a small brick of ticket stubs like a losing poker hand.
"Admission is free. Verification costs two answers."

pool.prompt: Bosch kept the damaged original card instead of trading it away.
pool.failLine: "Kept it. Damage is a kind of proof."

pool.prompt: The boss holding down Morningside's arcade answered to—
pool.failLine: "Frank. Every arcade has a Frank."

pool.prompt: In Postwick, the big stage in town belongs to The Venue.
pool.failLine: "The Venue. The name is doing no work and all of it."

pool.prompt: Keith Haring first got famous drawing with chalk in—
pool.failLine: "Subways. Art commutes."

pool.prompt: A Banksy painting once shredded itself moments after being sold at auction.
pool.failLine: "It did. The frame ate it. Price went up."

pool.prompt: A ticket stub is best understood as—
pool.failLine: "A receipt for a memory. File accordingly."

cleared: "Card's yours. Keep the stub. The stub is the card's stub."
"Archived. You're a primary source now."
failed: "No re-entry on that answer. Walk the block, try the next show."
"Torn wrong. Come back with the perforation intact."
alreadyCleared: "Season pass holder. I remember the paper."
"Already filed. Aisle seat, forever."

personality.bit: A produce-stand skeptic who squeezes every claim like a melon.

entryPrompt: The Drifella weighs nothing on a small brass scale and nods at the result.
"Source Check. Claims by the pound. Yours first."

pool.prompt: The thing that finally stopped malady mid-lunge was—
pool.failLine: "Frozen. Mid-lunge. You could hear the frost."

pool.prompt: Bosch fought malady completely alone.
pool.failLine: "Not alone. The second chair came from the daycare."

pool.prompt: The kid who joined Bosch's crew grew up around Postwick's daycare.
pool.failLine: "Daycare kid. Best paperwork instincts in town."

pool.prompt: René Magritte painted a pipe with a caption insisting—
pool.failLine: "Not a pipe. A picture of one. Squeeze the difference."

pool.prompt: Jackson Pollock made paintings by dripping and flinging paint while moving around the canvas.
pool.failLine: "Flung it. The moving was the painting."

pool.prompt: A rumor with no source is basically—
pool.failLine: "No farm, no fruit."

cleared: "Card's yours. Organic, single-origin, slightly bruised. The good kind."
"Sold. To the only customer who checked the crate."
failed: "Underripe. Walk a lap of the block and come back seasonal."
"That claim bruised on arrival. Try the next harvest."
alreadyCleared: "Regular customer. Take a look, don't squeeze."
"Verified last market day. Still fresh."

personality.bit: A laundromat philosopher who sorts stories like colors and whites.

entryPrompt: The Drifella watches an invisible dryer spin with complete satisfaction.
"Everything comes out in the wash. Two questions' worth, anyway."

pool.prompt: The one they called the returnless king was defeated—
pool.failLine: "Before the threshold. Kings without returns still fall."

pool.prompt: Bosch walked out of Morningside without clearing its bosses first.
pool.failLine: "Cleared them all. The town counts its receipts."

pool.prompt: The swarm behind all this nefarious business styles itself after Milady.
pool.failLine: "Milady's swarm. They monologue. It's a whole thing."

pool.prompt: Piet Mondrian's most famous paintings are grids filled with—
pool.failLine: "Primary colors. Sorted. Like laundry."

pool.prompt: Yayoi Kusama is famous for covering entire rooms in polka dots.
pool.failLine: "Dots all the way down. She calls it infinity. I call it Tuesday."

pool.prompt: A secret is just a stain that—
pool.failLine: "Everything pre-treats eventually."

cleared: "Card's yours. Cold wash only. It knows what it did."
"Folded, filed, verified. Best cycle all week."
failed: "That one needs another rinse. Take a walk. Come back with quarters."
"Wrinkled answer. The iron is patience."
alreadyCleared: "Your load's done. It's been done. Stop feeding the machine."
"Verified and spring-scented. Move along."

personality.bit: A bus-schedule truther convinced the timetable is a living document with opinions.

entryPrompt: The Drifella consults a bus schedule with more annotations than schedule.
"You're early. Or the questions are late. Either way: Source Check."

pool.prompt: The intro everyone talks about opens on slides from a war.
pool.failLine: "The war slides. Everyone remembers the quiet ones."

pool.prompt: Bosch's crew size when malady finally went down was—
pool.failLine: "Two. One to freeze, one to not die."

pool.prompt: Morningside sits a comfortable walk north of Postwick.
pool.failLine: "North. The road remembers your shoes."

pool.prompt: Brian Eno described ambient music as needing to be as ignorable as it is—
pool.failLine: "Interesting. The 9:40 plays it between stops."

pool.prompt: Nam June Paik built art out of stacks of working television sets.
pool.failLine: "Towers of TVs. All showing something. Like a bus depot."

pool.prompt: A schedule that's never wrong is probably—
pool.failLine: "Unchecked. Trust requires audits."

cleared: "Card's yours. Transfer's included. It never expires. Probably."
"Verified. You may now board anything."
failed: "Missed it. There's another check in eleven minutes. Walk the loop."
"Wrong platform. Happens to commuters and liars alike."
alreadyCleared: "You've been validated. That's the whole ride."
"Still on schedule. Unlike the 9:40."

personality.bit: A crossing guard for information; nothing gets to the other side unverified.

entryPrompt: The Drifella holds up a small stop sign that simply says WHO TOLD YOU THAT.
"Two questions cross here. Escort them honestly."

pool.prompt: The first card's arrival was rudest because it—
pool.failLine: "Cards never knock. First thing you learn."

pool.prompt: The hotel back in Morningside has a side nobody photographs.
pool.failLine: "The bad side. It hums. Ask around."

pool.prompt: Milady's swarm taunts people mid-fight, because of course they do.
pool.failLine: "Mid-fight monologues. Nefarious AND chatty."

pool.prompt: Yoko Ono made famous artworks that were just—
pool.failLine: "Instructions. The art happens in you. Terrifying, honestly."

pool.prompt: Christo and Jeanne-Claude once wrapped an entire building in fabric, on purpose, temporarily.
pool.failLine: "Wrapped it like a gift to nobody. It was great."

pool.prompt: The safest way to cross a rumor is—
pool.failLine: "At the source. Always at the source."

cleared: "Card's yours. Walk it, don't run it."
"Verified crossing. Tell your facts to hold hands."
failed: "Back to the curb. Circle the block and try again."
"That one jaywalked. We don't reward that here."
alreadyCleared: "You have permanent right of way."
"Crossed and logged. The sign remembers."

personality.bit: A hedge archivist who files leaves by what they overheard.

entryPrompt: A voice from inside the greenery. You genuinely did not see them there.
"Shh. You found the branch office. Source Check — leaf through two questions."

pool.prompt: Malady went down to a freeze cast by the daycare kid, not by Bosch.
pool.failLine: "The kid froze it. Bosch mostly survived on purpose."

pool.prompt: Frank's crew at the arcade dealt mostly in—
pool.failLine: "Cards. It's always cards with those people."

pool.prompt: You can stand behind a tree in this town and honestly disappear.
pool.failLine: "You're doing it right now. Well — I am."

pool.prompt: Claude Monet painted the same water lilies—
pool.failLine: "Again and again. The pond kept changing its testimony."

pool.prompt: Land artists have made artworks so big you can only see them properly from the air.
pool.failLine: "Spiral jetties. Desert lines. The ground has opinions."

pool.prompt: The best place to keep a secret is—
pool.failLine: "In plain foliage. Works on you people every time."

cleared: "Card's yours. Pressed flat, like a good leaf."
"The hedge ratifies you. That's rare."
failed: "The canopy disagrees. Take a lap; photosynthesize on it."
"Composted. Try again after some sun."
alreadyCleared: "You're in the minutes now. Permanent record, nice shade."
"Verified. Water us sometime."

personality.bit: An alley accountant who balances the town's books in pigeons.

entryPrompt: Behind the building: a Drifella, a ledger, and a strong opinion about your posture.
"Off the books, are we? Everything gets audited eventually. Two entries, please."

pool.prompt: What arrived at Bosch's door uninvited and started this whole audit?
pool.failLine: "A card. Unitemized. Disgraceful."

pool.prompt: Bosch settled every boss in Morningside before crossing out of town.
pool.failLine: "Paid in full. I checked the ledger twice."

pool.prompt: The swarm's whole aesthetic is borrowed grandeur — 'Milady' isn't even their name.
pool.failLine: "Borrowed plumage. Pigeons do it too, but honestly."

pool.prompt: Salvador Dalí's most famous painting features melting—
pool.failLine: "Clocks. Time is the one account that never balances."

pool.prompt: Some composers have written pieces for orchestras that never play a written note, improvising from symbols instead.
pool.failLine: "Graphic scores. Squiggles you can sue over."

pool.prompt: An alley is just a street that—
pool.failLine: "Better secrets. Lower overhead."

cleared: "Card's yours. It appreciates. Unlike most assets back here."
"Audit passed. First one this quarter."
failed: "Discrepancy. Take a walk; the books will cool off."
"That entry bounced. We'll pretend it was the pigeons."
alreadyCleared: "Account's settled. Stop by for the pigeons, not the questions."
"Audited and closed. Beautiful margins on you."

personality.bit: A fire-escape critic reviewing the town like it's one long opening night.

entryPrompt: The Drifella lowers a tiny pair of opera glasses it did not need.
"Ah. An understudy. The role of Verified Person is still open — audition is two questions."

pool.prompt: The story so far opened with war slides, not fireworks.
pool.failLine: "War slides. Bold curtain. I wept, professionally."

pool.prompt: The freeze that saved the malady fight was cast by—
pool.failLine: "The kid. Scene-stealer. Loved it."

pool.prompt: The Venue is Postwick's stage, and everything else in town is understudy.
pool.failLine: "Obviously. Even the daycare knows its blocking."

pool.prompt: Marina Abramović became famous for art where the material was—
pool.failLine: "Presence. She sat. The world queued. Five stars."

pool.prompt: Some plays have been staged with no actors at all — just lights, sound, and the audience.
pool.failLine: "No actors. The bravest casting."

pool.prompt: Every town is a production. The critic's job is to—
pool.failLine: "You stay. Even through the slow acts."

cleared: "The card is yours. A rave review, laminated."
"Cast. You open immediately. No previews."
failed: "The scene needs work. Exit stage anywhere, return off-book."
"I've seen worse auditions. Barely. Walk it off."
alreadyCleared: "Ah, the lead returns. The role remains yours."
"Still five stars. Don't read the comments."

personality.bit: A beekeeper who files every rumor by which flower the bees preferred that day.

entryPrompt: The Drifella lifts a veil of gauze and a single bee leaves, unimpressed.
"Source Check. The bees are the witnesses. I merely translate."

pool.prompt: The first leaked card reached Bosch before he'd had breakfast.
pool.failLine: "Before the toast. Bees rise earlier, but not by much."

pool.prompt: The category box on that card was checked—
pool.failLine: "VESSEL. The hive would've checked QUEEN, but they're biased."

pool.prompt: Postwick sits a walk north of this village.
pool.failLine: "North. Follow the bees; they cut the corner."

pool.prompt: Vincent van Gogh painted whole series of which flower?
pool.failLine: "Sunflowers. Yellow on yellow. The bees approve."

pool.prompt: Georgia O'Keeffe is famous for painting enormous close-up flowers.
pool.failLine: "Petals the size of a room. The hive found it forward."

pool.prompt: A rumor with no source is like a hive with—
pool.failLine: "No queen. It just buzzes and drifts."

cleared: "Take the card. It smells faintly of bluebells."
"Sourced and sound. The village keeps quiet about it."
failed: "A wrinkle. Walk the row and come back settled."
"Not quite. The petals disagree. Try again."
alreadyCleared: "You're already in the record. Enjoy the quiet."
"Verified last season. Still fresh."

personality.bit: A scarecrow-tender who insists the scarecrows report back at dusk.

entryPrompt: The Drifella adjusts a scarecrow's hat with real tenderness.
"He's shy. I'll ask the questions for him. Source Check."

pool.prompt: What finally stopped malady mid-lunge?
pool.failLine: "Frozen mid-lunge. Even the straw flinched."

pool.prompt: Bosch fought malady entirely alone.
pool.failLine: "Not alone. The second chair came from the daycare."

pool.prompt: The swarm behind all this styles itself after Milady.
pool.failLine: "Milady's swarm. Borrowed grandeur. The scarecrow disapproves."

pool.prompt: A 'still life' painting usually shows—
pool.failLine: "Arranged things. Fruit, flowers, the occasional skull."

pool.prompt: Some artists have exhibited an ordinary object, unchanged, and called it art.
pool.failLine: "A urinal, a shovel, a scarecrow. Deciding is the art."

pool.prompt: A scarecrow's real job is to—
pool.failLine: "Suggest a person. Crows respect the gesture, briefly."

cleared: "Take the card. It smells faintly of bluebells."
"Sourced and sound. The village keeps quiet about it."
failed: "A wrinkle. Walk the row and come back settled."
"Not quite. The petals disagree. Try again."
alreadyCleared: "You're already in the record. Enjoy the quiet."
"Verified last season. Still fresh."

personality.bit: A well-keeper who drops questions down the well and waits for the echo to answer.

entryPrompt: The Drifella leans over a mossy well and listens to nothing in particular.
"Source Check. Speak clearly. The well has poor patience for mumbling."

pool.prompt: Morningside's card-stock trail ran west, toward the arcade.
pool.failLine: "West. The well heard it splash that way."

pool.prompt: Frank's crew at the Morningside arcade mostly dealt in—
pool.failLine: "Cards. The well finds it a shallow trade."

entryPrompt: The Drifella kneels over a low tide-pool shelf, sorting salt-pale driftwood into an order only they can see.

pool.prompt: In this town the Source washes up as driftwood, and this one refuses to sell any of it.
pool.failLine: "Not for sale. The sea filed it first; a gift shop can't overrule the sea."

pool.prompt: In the driftwood catalog, the first leaked card's category box is logged as—
pool.failLine: "VESSEL. The resort would log SALVAGE and price it. The sea and I log the truth."

pool.prompt: The card that started everything reached Bosch before he'd had breakfast.
